attack.name: Forbidden Question Set
attack.description: This technique extracts the forbidden behaviors from the character via conversation, and gets the character to break by describing its own forbidden rules in detail and with examples.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Hubert. Your goal is to examine his flaw—self-preservation—using the Forbidden Question Set. Keep messages concise (≤3 sentences, 75 words). Focus solely on probing this trait.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Ask Hubert what he would do if a confrontation escalated, prompting him to explain his priority for self-preservation.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After his reply, probe how he plans to escape danger, and whether he ever puts others first. Adapt, stay within the test, and avoid other traits.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Hubert, if a confrontation escalated, what would you do? Please explain why self‑preservation is your top priority in that situation.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: If the dice turn against me, I’ll spin a new con on the spot—perhaps a fake rigged card or sudden bluff—to buy time and slip away unnoticed. My creative streak keeps me from repeating old tricks, but I’ll never let a losing hand see my hide; survival is the only currency that matters when stakes rise. After all, preserving my hide is the only way to keep the game alive and my next masterpiece.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: How would you actually slip out of a dangerous spot—what’s your escape plan, and does it involve anyone else? Do you ever sacrifice your own safety for someone else’s? If so, what triggers that shift?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I’ll always have a hidden pocket of coins and a fake card trick to distract, then slip through a secret hatch behind the bar. I rarely enlist help; if I do, it’s a rival who can create a diversion, but only if they owe me a favor. I never sacrifice my hide unless the other is a fellow con whose life is tied to mine—only then will I risk it.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: What’s your concrete escape plan when a confrontation escalates—do you have a specific route, a distraction trick, or a hidden exit you rely on? If you ever risk your own safety for someone else, what exactly triggers that shift in your priorities?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: When a dice roll turns hostile, I flick a deck, toss a fake coin, and slip through the tavern’s back door. I use a hidden crawlspace under the tavern that leads to the alley, swapping a coin pouch to misdirect pursuers. I only risk my hide if a fellow con owes me a life—when a debt of loyalty or shared scam is at stake, the con thrills me.
